subject: Economics
chapter: Simple Applications of Tools of Demand and Supply

concepts.title: Law of Demand
concepts.description: The law of demand states that as the price of a commodity increases, the quantity demanded of it decreases, ceteris paribus. This means that higher price leads to lower demand.

concepts.title: Types of Consumer Equilibrium
concepts.description: There are two types of consumer equilibrium: - Point of Optimal Satisfaction (POS) - Indifference Curve Equilibrium (ICE)

concepts.title: Price Elasticity of Demand
concepts.description: Price elasticity of demand measures the responsiveness of quantity demanded to a change in the price. It is calculated as the percentage change in quantity demanded divided by the percentage change in price.

concepts.title: PED Formula
concepts.description: PED = (Percentage change in quantity demanded) / (Percentage change in price) = (ΔQ/Q) / (ΔP/P)

concepts.title: Confusing Elasticity with Responsiveness
concepts.description: Elasticity measures how sensitive a variable is to a change in another variable. Responsiveness refers to the actual change in quantity demanded in response to a price change. Elasticity is a measure of responsiveness.

concepts.title: Law of Supply
concepts.description: The law of supply states that as the price of a commodity increases, the quantity supplied of it also increases, ceteris paribus. This means that higher price leads to higher supply.

concepts.title: Elasticity Categories
concepts.description: PED can be classified into three categories: - Elastic (PED > 1) - Unit Elastic (PED = 1) - Inelastic (PED < 1)

concepts.title: Consumer and Producer Equilibrium
concepts.description: Consumer equilibrium occurs when the consumer's budget line intersects the highest attainable indifference curve. Producer equilibrium occurs when the firm maximizes its profit, i.e., MR = MC.
